title: Drip vs Flodesk (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip vs Flodesk email marketing tools. See pricing, features, ratings and which fits your business best.
intro: Drip and Flodesk are both capable email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Drip focuses on e-commerce integration and revenue tracking, while Flodesk emphasizes beautiful design and unlimited subscriber scaling. Choosing between them depends on whether you prioritize store connectivity or visual aesthetics.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce businesses with store integration needs

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce business and need deep shopping cart integration and revenue tracking
Revenue attribution and sales performance metrics are critical to your marketing decisions
You want SMS capabilities built into your email platform
choose_b_if: You prioritize stunning, design-forward email templates and visual branding
You have a growing subscriber list and want unlimited contacts included in your plan
You need a flat monthly rate without worrying about subscriber scaling costs

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Drip excels for e-commerce businesses requiring advanced tracking and store integration, while Flodesk is superior for visual brands prioritizing design quality and unlimited growth. Flodesk has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.8 G2, 4.7 Trustpilot) compared to Drip (4.4 G2, 3.5 Trustpilot).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Flodesk?
faq.a: Flodesk offers better value for growing businesses with its flat $38/month rate that includes unlimited subscribers. Drip starts at $39/month but scales with subscriber count, potentially becoming more expensive as you grow. For small lists, both are comparable; for large subscriber bases, Flodesk wins.

faq.q: Can I switch from Flodesk to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support standard email export formats, making migration possible. You can export your subscriber lists and campaign data from Flodesk and import them into Drip. Most reputable email platforms provide migration assistance or documentation to minimize setup time during the switch.
